title: Phase 1 Any-Cleanup Architecture Plan — packages/opencode/src
summary: Replace 34 `any` annotations with specific types: catch clauses → unknown, callback params → inline object types, local variables → inferred or concrete types.

root_causes.hypothesis: Lax catch clause typing — TypeScript 4.0+ allows `unknown` in catch but many catch blocks predate this convention
evidence: 7 catch-clause any annotations: session/llm.ts L143, mcp/index.ts L217, mcp/index.ts L804

root_causes.hypothesis: Untyped callback parameters in filter/map chains — developers used `any` for convenience when the shape is knowable from surrounding code
evidence: 15+ callback any annotations: smart-git.ts L152-154, smart-bun.ts L105, task-board.ts L148, copilot.ts L107-140, transform.ts L316-317, etc.

root_causes.hypothesis: Untyped Drizzle query results — `any[]` used where Drizzle row types or local mapping types would suffice
evidence: coordination.ts L636, L639; claims.ts L126, L127, L177-178

root_causes.hypothesis: Missing local interface definitions for worker event data
evidence: validation-parser-worker.ts L9, L23, L34, L90 — all use `event: any` where a TestEvent interface would work

fixes.description: Replace `catch (e: any)` with `catch (e: unknown)` and add instanceof Error guards for .message access

changes.after:
catch: (e: unknown) => {
          const errMsg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : undefined
          log.error(`failed to ${label}`, { clientName, ...meta, error: errMsg })
          return e

risks.detail: mcp/index.ts L217 catch returns `e` directly — with `unknown` this may propagate unknown to callers of fetchFromClient
mcp/index.ts L804 uses optional chaining `e?.message` — with `unknown` this won't compile; replaced with explicit guard

validation: Verify no .message access on unknown slips through

fixes.description: Replace `any` callback params with inline object types matching the data shape from surrounding code

changes.after: output.staged_count = files.filter((f: { staged: boolean; unstaged: boolean; status: string }) => f.staged).length
output.unstaged_count = files.filter((f: { staged: boolean; unstaged: boolean; status: string }) => f.unstaged).length
output.untracked_count = files.filter((f: { staged: boolean; unstaged: boolean; status: string }) => f.status === "??").length

changes.before: const files = new Set(errors.map((e: any) => e.file))
changes.after:
type TypecheckError = { file: string; line: number; col: number; level: string; code: string; message: string }
            const files = new Set(errors.map((e: TypecheckError) => e.file))
tool_count: new Set(phases.map((p: { tool: string }) => p.tool)).size,
sessionClaims.map((r: Parameters<typeof claimRowToType>[0]) => formatStructuredResult(claimRowToType(r)))
...runningClaims.map((c: { task_id: string; description: string }) => `    ${c.task_id}: ${c.description}`)
const reasoningParts = msg.content.filter((part: { type: string }) => part.type === "reasoning")
const reasoningText = reasoningParts.map((part: { text: string }) => part.text).join("")

changes.before: result.required = result.required.filter((field: any) => field in result.properties)
changes.after: result.required = result.required.filter((field: string) => field in result.properties)
msg.content.some((part: { type: string }) => part.type === "image_url")
item.content.some((part: { type: string }) => part.type === "input_image")
last.content.some((part: { type?: string }) => part?.type !== "tool_result")
(part: { type: string; content?: unknown }) =>
part.content.some((nested: { type?: string }) => nested?.type === "image")

changes.before: const asset = release.assets.find((a: any) => a.name === assetName)
changes.after: const asset = release.assets.find((a: { name: string }) => a.name === assetName)

risks.detail: Inline types may be slightly wider/narrower than actual data shapes. These are pure filter/map closures — only accessed fields matter.
coordination.ts L636 uses Parameters<> utility — requires claimRowToType import. Already imported locally, no change needed.

validation: Manually verify filter callback types don't cause downstream assignment errors

fixes.description: Replace `any` in local variable declarations and function signatures with concrete types

changes.after: let resolve: () => void
const txFn = async (tx: DrizzleLikeClient) => {
options["fetch"] = async (input: RequestInfo | URL, init?: BunFetchRequestInit) => {

changes.before: function safeBind(sql: string, params: any[]): string {
changes.after: function safeBind(sql: string, params: unknown[]): string {
): Effect.Effect<void> {
Effect.map((exit: Effect.Effect.Exit<unknown, unknown>) => {
const allClaims: Parameters<typeof claimRowToType>[0][] = yield* adapter.query((db) =>

risks.detail: provider/provider.ts L1633: RequestInfo | URL may not be available in all TS configs. `RequestInfo` is a lib.dom type. Alternative: use `string | URL` if DOM types aren't loaded.

tool/typed-result.ts L505: Effect.Effect.Exit<unknown, unknown> is imprecise but safer than any. The function already has explicit type casts elsewhere; a more precise generic would require restructuring the function.
tool/coordination.ts L639: Parameters<typeof claimRowToType>[0][] relies on claimRowToType being in scope (it is, defined at L132).
storage/db.duckdb.ts L121: changing any[] to unknown[] in safeBind — the function body accesses params via indexing and typeof checks, which work fine with unknown.

validation: Check provider.ts for DOM type availability

fixes.description: Define a local TestEvent interface and replace 4 `any` params in validation-parser-worker.ts

changes.added_type:
interface TestEvent {
  payloadJson?: Record<string, unknown>
  errorMessage?: string
  filePath?: string
  eventType?: string
  sessionId?: string
  ts?: string
}

risks.detail: New TestEvent interface is local-only (not exported). The functions access properties with optional chaining and typeof guards already, so adding the interface won't change runtime behavior.
The events array comes from eventStore.query() which returns unknown shape — the TestEvent interface is a structural subset, not an exhaustive type.

fixes.description: Define ClaimSummary and PathTreeNode types in claims.ts and replace 5 `any` annotations

before_lines.177: function convert(node: Record<string, any>, parentPath: string): any[] {

after_lines.177: function convert(node: Record<string, { file?: { path: string; name: string; type: "file"; status: string; claim?: ClaimSummary }; dir?: { children: Record<string, unknown>; claim?: ClaimRow; status?: string } }>, parentPath: string): PathTreeNode[] {

risks.detail: The convert() function works with a recursive tree structure built dynamically. The proposed node type for the first param (Record<string, { file?, dir? }>) approximates the shape but may need refinement after typecheck. Consider a more precise recursive type.
ClaimRow is a subset of the Drizzle row type — may need to derive from typeof CoordinationClaimTable for full accuracy in a later phase.

validation: Verify tree endpoint still returns correct structure

rollback_plan: Revert each fix in reverse order: E → D → C → B → A. Each fix touches non-overlapping files except coordination.ts (shared between B and C). For coordination.ts, revert the later fix first (C) then B.

post_apply: bun typecheck from packages/opencode (verify zero regressions)
validation_strategy.smoke_test: bun test from packages/opencode — test-name-pattern for affected modules
validation_strategy.bisect_strategy: Apply fixes one file at a time, re-running typecheck after each

notes.discrepancy: Inventory overall count says 33 EASY but by_file scanning found 34 entries. The extra entry may be a counting artifact. All 34 are planned.
notes.deferred_to_phase2: The claims.ts `convert()` node param type may need a fully recursive type definition; the current plan uses a pragmatic one-level type. If typecheck fails, a recursive TreeNode = { [key: string]: FileNode | DirNode } is the fallback.

tool/coordination.ts L639 uses Parameters<> utility which depends on a function signature. If claimRowToType's signature changes in a future phase, this will break at typecheck time — which is the desired behavior.
provider/provider.ts L1633: if RequestInfo is not available, fall back to `string | URL`.
